works.title: Fitbit Enterprise
works.summary: Server-first B2B marketing platform for Google's enterprise health division. Sole frontend lead across two architectural phases, ending in a 0kB JavaScript, Preact-powered experience handed off directly to Google.
highlights: Sole frontend lead on a two-phase architectural rebuild: WordPress + Gutenberg → Next.js + Contentful → Root.js (server-first Preact framework) for a fully server-rendered, 0kB JS architecture
Replaced Gutenberg blocks with a headless Contentful CMS, giving Google's team full content ownership without engineering involvement
Full site including blog migrated and delivered across both phases
works.impact: Full B2B marketing + blog site delivered across two architectural phases and handed off to Google

works.title: iFit
works.summary: SSR marketing infrastructure for a global fitness platform with 6M+ members. Frontend lead on a full server-rendered site and blog built with Next.js and Contentstack.
highlights: Built the full SSR infrastructure and blog section from scratch — hundreds of posts migrated and served as individual server-rendered pages
Headless CMS integration with Contentstack giving the marketing team full content autonomy without engineering involvement
100% Lighthouse score, WCAG AAA accessibility across all pages
Google Analytics implementation for marketing and conversion tracking
works.impact: SSR infrastructure serving a platform with 6M+ global members

works.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
works.client: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
works.summary: Consumer museum site on Next.js and headless WordPress. Led accessibility implementation to WCAG AAA and Section 508 — a standard above the legal requirement, chosen as a deliberate craft decision.
highlights: Led end-to-end WCAG AAA and Section 508 remediation across all site components — above the legally required AA standard
Accessibility work extended to history.org as part of the same engagement
Part of a team that achieved perfect Lighthouse performance scores
works.impact: Full site WCAG AAA compliant across all components

projects.summary: Rebuilding GEICO's dotcom from scratch, starting from their internal brand guidelines and building the design system itself — the component library that powers the new site.
highlights: Built a new enterprise design system from GEICO's internal brand guidelines up
Rebuilding geico.com using the component library, closing the loop between system and product
Components documented and published via Storybook for team-wide consumption

projects.summary: High-density data UI for enterprise financial monitoring. Built Lit web components and a React dashboard for internal AML compliance tooling at one of the largest US banks.
highlights: Designed and built data table components in Lit for high-density compliance data display
React dashboard for internal AML monitoring and reporting
Enterprise-grade component architecture for sensitive financial tooling
